sentence_reviews.text: Runtime assurance monitors a deployed system and blocks, overrides, degrades, or transfers control when behavior approaches a defined safety boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey and NIST AI RMF support monitoring, safety constraints, runtime risk controls, and fallback/mitigation concepts for deployed systems.
sentence_reviews.notes: Definition is well supported as synthesis; exact block/override/degrade list is not a single cited claim.

sentence_reviews.text: It provides protection when correctness cannot be fully established before deployment.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey discusses limits of verification/specification; NIST emphasizes monitoring and risk management across lifecycle/deployment.
Formal ML survey covers safety verification/assurance for ML in control/CPS contexts; AIMA supports robotics/control foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported as a runtime assurance example, though exact phrase is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports monitoring and controls, but the packet lacks a specific tool-call monitor source.
Formal ML/control context and AIMA control foundations support fallback or supervisory control ideas at a high level.

sentence_reviews.text: Monitors can miss unmodeled hazards, trigger too late, or become a single point of failure.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal ML survey discusses specification/model limitations; NIST supports residual risk and monitoring limitations in governance.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported as a risk synthesis; single-point-of-failure detail is plausible but not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Runtime assurance, monitors & safety envelopes measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Formal verification of agents & plans.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Formal methods specify and verify properties that can inform runtime safety envelopes and monitors.

sentence_reviews.text: Runtime assurance, monitors & safety envelopes checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Human-in-the-loop, permissions & sandboxing.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports human oversight, accountability, controls, and monitoring, but not sandboxing/tool-permission mechanisms specifically.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship type is plausible; core strength is undersourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Runtime assurance, monitors & safety envelopes measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF emphasizes monitoring, measurement, and managing AI risks throughout the lifecycle.

sentence_reviews.text: Runtime assurance, monitors & safety envelopes measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports control theory foundations; formal ML survey covers safety/verification for learned control systems.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is defensible for safety envelopes and fallback control.

edge_reviews.notes: Formal verification/specifications can define runtime safety envelopes; core strength is defensible.
Human oversight and controls are supported, but sandboxing/permission verification is not directly established as core.
Continuous monitoring is a common runtime assurance dependency.
Control theory is relevant to safety envelopes and fallback controllers; common strength is appropriate.
